sentiment.rationale: Haggai rebukes the people for living in paneled houses while the Lord's house lies waste; God calls them to consider their ways and build the temple.
God promises the glory of the latter house shall exceed the former; He will shake the heavens and earth, and Zerubbabel is chosen as a signet.

missingCharacters.name: Haggai
missingCharacters.description: A post-exilic prophet who urged Zerubbabel and Joshua to rebuild the temple and promised that its future glory would exceed the former.

summaries.summary: Haggai rebukes the returned exiles for neglecting the temple while building their own houses; the people obey, and the Lord stirs their spirit to begin rebuilding.
God encourages the builders that the latter temple's glory will exceed Solomon's; a lesson on holiness and defilement is given, and Zerubbabel is chosen as God's signet.

crossReferences.note: The author of Hebrews quotes God's promise to shake the heavens and the earth once more.
The glory of the latter house — LDS understanding connects this to latter-day temple building.
Zerubbabel chosen as a signet ring — Zechariah also affirms Zerubbabel's role in completing the temple.

verses.reason: 'Is it time for you, O ye, to dwell in your cieled houses, and this house lie waste?' — a pointed rebuke on priorities.
'Thus saith the Lord of hosts; Consider your ways' — a repeated call to self-examination.
'The glory of this latter house shall be greater than of the former... and in this place will I give peace' — a grand promise of future temple glory.
